title: The Money Zone Method: What It Is and How It Works
subtitle: An overview of the energetic framework behind the release-and-allow practice in this library
excerpt: An overview of the Money Zone method, the energetic framework behind the release-and-allow practices in this library. Explains what the Zone is, why the standard mindset tools often don't stick, and how the method differs from affirmation and journalling alone.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024). Summarised for homemade.education.

content.text: An overview of the framework behind the energy statements, rituals, and release practices in this library. You don't need to read this before using those practices, but it explains why they're built the way they are.
Why the standard tools often don't stick

Affirmations, journalling, vision boards, and positive thinking all have value. The problem is that they work at the level of conscious intention, while the patterns driving your money behaviour often sit at a deeper level. You can say the right thing out loud and believe almost none of it underneath. That gap is where most mindset work falls short.

What's missing is a way to locate the specific resistance that's running, release it at the level it's held, and replace it with a different state. The body state needs to shift alongside the thought.

The Zone is the energetic state where what you want and what you're holding inside are in alignment. In a money context: the Money Zone is the state where the feelings, beliefs, and internal patterns you carry match the frequency of money flowing. Not striving, not bracing. Aligned.

Everyone has been in something close to this state at some point, a period when money felt easier, when income moved without force, when the anxiety about it dropped. That's not luck. It's alignment. The method is a way of returning to that state deliberately.
The method works in two steps: release and allow.

Releasing clears out what's in the way. You identify a specific belief, pattern, or feeling that's running at a misaligned frequency, and you actively discharge it. Not push it down, not argue with it. Release it.
Allowing sets the new state. Once the old pattern is released, you explicitly invite in the aligned state. Not assert it into being, not fake it. Allow it.
The energy statements, rituals, and release exercises in this library are built on this two-step shape. The wording matters. Repetition in threes matters. So does the present-tense form.

This method doesn't replace practical financial action. It works alongside it. The inner shift removes the patterns that keep you stuck, sabotaging, or braced. The outer action is still yours to take. The combination of both is where things actually move.
Drawn from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024). Summarised for homemade.education as a basic guide to the release-and-allow shape.
